name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 86 of Hormuz blockade (Sunday May 24 — markets closed). **BRENT $103.54 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (-5%+ ON THE WEEK) IS THE CLEANEST PAPER-SIDE DEAL-ANTICIPATION PRINT OF THE CYCLE. WTI $96.60 (-8%+ ON THE WEEK). INTRADAY BRENT TRADED AS HIGH AS $106 BEFORE FADING ON RUBIO + TRUMP DEAL-PROGRESS REMARKS. THE STRUCTURAL PHYSICAL PREMIUM (-36% HORMUZ VOLUME, AWRP 1%, VLCC $100K/DAY) IS INTACT, BUT FUTURES ARE PRICING THE DEAL TRACK AT INCREASINGLY HIGH PROBABILITY. THE ASYMMETRIC SET-UP INTO MONDAY: IF DEAL TEXT EMERGES WITH IRAN'S HORMUZ-MANAGEMENT LANGUAGE INTACT (PER FARS REBUTTAL), BRENT COULD GAP +$5-8 ON DEAL-FADE; IF A CLEAN DISMANTLEMENT FRAMEWORK IS ANNOUNCED, BRENT COULD GAP -$8-15 TOWARD THE $93-97 TRUMP-XI-DELIVERABLE TRIGGER BAND. SUNDAY TRUMP 'TIME IS ON OUR SIDE' PIVOT HAS LIKELY ALREADY REMOVED THE GAP-DOWN CASE FOR MONDAY'S ASIA OPEN — DEAL-FAST PATH IS NOW STRUCTURALLY LESS LIKELY.** Per markets dossier C1 + intel B6: paper market is pricing the deal at high probability; physical market is pricing zero operational change — textbook 'paper-physical divergence' trade. **Reversibility**: HIGH for paper component if deal fails; LOW for physical premium (insurance/shipping/TTF/helium take 6-12 weeks to unwind even on clean deal). **Helima Croft framing dominant in oil-strategist commentary**: 'The fine print of the agreement will matter. If Iran continues to have the final say on passage, some insurers and shippers may be reluctant to rush back in.' Brent +56% above $73 baseline → tail-risk de-priced ~$7-8 in last week; structural premium ~$30 intact. **Trigger watch**: 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' = Goldman $120 Q3 base case mathematically foreclosed (Day 0 of 3, BELOW threshold via paper-side; physical-side test pending). PRIOR (May 23): BRENT $103.54 MAY 22 CLOSE (+0.9% 1D, -1.5% 1M, +44.8% vs $73 baseline) — FLAT INTO WEEKEND DESPITE HORMUZ DAY 85 + IRGC 25-VESSEL CLAIM. DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE FULLY MATURED. THE COMBINED MAY 23 INPUT SET (TRUMP 'EVERY DAY BETTER' + 'SATISFACTORILY HANDLED' URANIUM + MUNIR COAS IN TEHRAN + CBS MILITARY MEMORIAL-DAY CANCELLATION + IRANIAN INSTITUTIONAL COUNTER-SIGNAL FANOUT) RESOLVES INTO A MARKET THAT IS EXPLICITLY NOT PRICING MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW.** Brent is now FLAT at $103-104 across the weekend; WTI $96.60 +0.3% 1D, +0.8% MoM; structural premium ~$30+ intact above $73 baseline; tail premium compressed ~$7-8 vs May 18-19 peak. **Croft 'Tuesday close vs $112' sustain-rule binary remains FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE confirmed**; StanChart $130-145 path momentum extinguished; Goldman $120 Q3 base case mathematically foreclosed if Brent closes below $100 sustained 3+ sessions (Day 0 of 3). **Saturday's diplomatic input is dominantly bearish for tail-risk**: Trump's 'every day better,' Munir COAS escalation, Baghaei 30-60 day timeline anchor longer-horizon deal; CBS Memorial Day weekend US military cancellation is the bullish counter-vector but markets discounted it (gold flat at $4,521, VIX cycle low 16.70). **Iranian counter-signal at maximum amplitude** (Ghalibaf 'US not honest' + UN mission 'excessive demands' + Tasnim 'Iran preparing for resuming fighting') reads as institutional posturing inside Munir window, not as pre-kinetic positioning. **The May 24 strike-window operational binary tomorrow**: any Sunday-night kinetic event would put Brent +$10-15 toward $115-120, VIX +5-8 pts, gold +$200-500; deal-acceptance announcement would put Brent -$5-8 toward $96-99 and break sub-$100 trigger. **Trigger: 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' — Day 0 of 3**, watching. **India fuel pass-through cascade live**: third petrol/diesel hike May 23 in 8 days (cumulative ₹4.74 petrol, ₹4.82 diesel) — Indian crude basket averaging $113-114/bbl post-war vs $69 pre-war; OMC pass-through now durable. **Physical-paper bifurcation persists**: Frontline Q2 contracted TCE $181,700/day on 82% of VLCC days = physical book locked regardless of spot; paper market sold the Q2 ballast-day guide (-3.4% 1D). **WTI broke $100 to $96.60** is meaningful psychological breach. PRIOR (May 22): **CROFT 'TUESDAY CLOSE VS $112' BINARY RESOLVES TO FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE — BRENT $104.52 MAY 22 (-5.3% CUMULATIVE WINDOW VS $110.32 MAY 19); WTI BROKE $100 TO $99.50 (-6% TWO SESSIONS). MAY 21-22 CLOSES DECISIVELY BELOW $108 MATHEMATICALLY FALSIFY THE MAY 18-19 2-SESSION SUSTAIN RULE. STANCHART $130-145 PATH MOMENTUM EXTINGUISHED. GOLDMAN $120 Q3 BASE ITSELF RE-PRICING. STRUCTURAL PREMIUM INTACT AT $30+ ABOVE $73 BASELINE — TAIL-RISK DE-PRICING ~$7-8, NOT STRUCTURAL DE-PRICING.** Brent settled $104.52 May 22 (-5.25% vs $110.32 May 19, -6% cumulative two-session move per Energy Intelligence, Fortune, CNBC); WTI broke $100 to ~$99.50 May 21 (Energy Intelligence). **The Brent breaks $108-110 sustained 2 sessions trigger fire from May 18-19 closes IS NOW MATHEMATICALLY FALSIFIED as a sustain-rule positive**: May 21-22 closes decisively below $108 → revert to one-session positive. **The Croft binary resolves toward the FALSE-POSITIVE fire path her May 19 framing anticipated**; StanChart $130-145 escalation path loses durable momentum. **Goldman's $120 Q3 / $115 Q4 base case (if Hormuz remains mostly shut) is ALSO re-pricing** because Brent below $105 is below their conditional Hormuz-shut base; market is pricing toward 'Hormuz partially reopens within 1-2 months' optionality. **But Brent at $104.52 is still 43% above the $73 pre-crisis baseline** — the deal-anticipation re-price has compressed the tail premium ~$7-8 while preserving the structural premium ~$30 above baseline. **Primary chain (crisis-linked ~80%)**: Pakistan-mediated draft text leaked via Al Arabiya late May 21 (REPORTED) → Trump publicly pivoted from 'an hour away' (May 18) to 'in no hurry' + Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do' → DNI Gabbard resigned May 22 (second senior Iran-skeptic IC departure of cycle) → markets price elevated probability of Hormuz partial reopen within 1-2 months → tail-risk premium compresses ~$7-8/bbl. **Counter-vector (~20% structural)**: Mojtaba Khamenei's first policy directive (leaked May 21) — enriched uranium stays in Iran — forecloses Trump's May 16 letter nuclear basis; leaked Al Arabiya text addresses Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions but NOT uranium dismantlement; asymmetric path: ceasefire deliverable in days, nuclear deliverable foreclosed; Brent at $104.52 still 43% above baseline → structural premium $30+ intact. **TTF gas €47.69/MWh (-3.48% 1D / +7.38% 1M / +30.7% YoY)** — Norway Troll maintenance eased; GCC LNG halt persists; shallow retracement despite Brent -5.3% = European energy market pricing nuclear-track unresolved + Qatar Ras Laffan 17% force majeure as structural. **WTI-Brent spread compresses to ~$5** (vs ~$6.75 May 19) — consistent with tail-risk-de-pricing dominating. **Assessment**: TAIL-RISK DE-PRICING dominant, STRUCTURAL DE-PRICING NOT YET PRICED. If Pakistan deal text holds and partial Hormuz reopens, Brent gaps -$10-15 to $89-94. If Israel-unilateral kinetic action surfaces (Bibi 'hair on fire' framing per Axios May 20), Brent gaps +$8-15 toward $112-120. **WTI broke $100 to $99.50 is a meaningful psychological breach** — May 8-9 OPEC+ quota increase to 188K bpd June implementation not formally reversed even as Iranian/OPEC member capacity remains offline; UAE LEAVING OPEC May 1 (encoded May 19) removes 3M+ bpd theoretical spare capacity from cartel's spare-capacity arithmetic (EIA cut OPEC 2027 spare capacity forecast to 2.5M b/d from 3.8M b/d). **Physical-vs-paper bifurcation persists**: paper-crude re-prices the postponement-plus-deal-anticipation tail; physical-shipping rates (Frontline Q2 TCE $181.7k/day) confirm physical premium intact. **NEW TRIGGER added: 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions (intra-cycle psychological + Iranian-pricing floor)'** — watching Day 0 of 3; if fired, Goldman's Q3 $120 base case is mathematically foreclosed; JPM's $60 2026 average comes back into the band. PRIOR (May 19): **STANCHART $130-145 PATH ACTIVATES — 2-SESSION SUSTAIN RULE FIRES.** Brent $111.47 +2.0% Monday May 18 (intraday range $110.13-$111.99 per Investing/Barchart) + Friday May 15 confirmed close $109.26 = **two consecutive closes above $108 trigger threshold met.** Trigger added May 12 ('Brent breaks $108-110 sustained 2 sessions') moves from watching → ACTIVE_FIRED. Cascade now activates to inr-usd (₹96 trigger fires same day — see below), nifty-50, sp-500, marine-war-risk-insurance, energy-sector. WTI live Monday $108.20 +2.6% per OilPrice/Investing (yfinance $103.41 -1.9% is stale-comparator artifact; Brent-WTI spread COMPRESSED to ~$3-4, not widened). **MONDAY MAY 18 CLOSE ABOVE $108 = 2-SESSION-SUSTAIN RULE MET = STANCHART $130-145 PATH ACTIVATES.** Per yfinance closes (BZ=F): May 12 $107.77 / May 13 $105.63 / May 14 $105.72 / May 15 $109.26 — only May 15 is a confirmed close above $108. **Monday May 18 close is the binary test**: above $108 = rule met → StanChart $130-145 path activates → cascade to inr-usd, nifty-50, sp-500, marine-war-risk-insurance. Below $108 = trinary $100-110 band holds against the strongest escalation signal of the cycle. **NEW WEEKEND OVERLAY**: NYT reported Israel + US 'most intense preparations yet to renew attacks on Iran possibly as early as next week' (REPORTED) including commando operation for nuclear material and Kharg Island ground option; Trump posted 'Calm Before the Storm' Truth Social image (CONFIRMED RHETORIC); Iran REPORTEDLY moved additional air defenses to Kharg Island; Pakistan-Naqvi mediation trip to Tehran simultaneously running (CONFIRMED ACTION). **REVERSIBILITY ASYMMETRIC**: Iran has 2 months under Trump letter. If Shamkhani-frame materializes (sanctions-for-dismantling), Brent gaps -$8-12 to $93-97 within 5 sessions. If Iran rejects formally and NYT preparation operationalizes into kinetic action, Brent gaps +$8-15 toward $130-145. **PRIOR (May 16):** BRENT $109.26 +3.3% 1D / +9.9% 1M / +62.1% 3M; WTI $105.42 +4.2% 1D / +11.3% 1M / +69.1% 3M (WTI booked +11% weekly gain — cleanest week of physical-tightness reflection in cycle)**. **PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION RESOLVED VIOLENTLY INTO PHYSICAL CONFIRMATION on May 15**: yesterday's paper-Brent $107.42 close + crisis-trade unwind has reversed cleanly — paper-equity capitulated, yields surged, oil ripped. **First close above $108 since the trigger was added — May 15 $109.26 (single-session). Per yfinance closes, May 12 $107.77, May 13 $105.63, May 14 $105.72 were all below $108; the 2-session-of-closes sustain rule is NOT yet met. Trigger stays WATCHING; StanChart $130-145 path stays DORMANT pending Monday May 18 close.** Primary chain (~85% crisis-linked): (1) **Trump-Xi summit produced joint statement only, no HEU-custodian operational deliverable**; (2) **Trump immediately reverted to escalatory rhetoric via Fox/Hannity/Truth Social** ('decimation to be continued' + 'not patient'); (3) **Trump on May 16 sent Iran a new nuclear ultimatum letter demanding full dismantling + halt all enrichment + end proxy support within 2 months** (CONFIRMED letter sent; CLAIMED contents); (4) **Iran denied receiving any new proposal** but Khamenei-advisor Shamkhani offered conditional acceptance on full sanctions removal; FM Araghchi told Tasnim enrichment 'not on the agenda' but 'addressed in later stages'; (5) **Hormuz transit per Iran's own claim ~30 vessels/day vs 140 pre-war** — Iran allowed 30+ Chinese ships since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols,' partially explaining the Aramco ~30/day benchmark; (6) **TTF Gas €49.86/MWh +4.63% to highest since April** = physical confirmation LNG complex feels the squeeze. Secondary contributor (~15%): refinery utilization seasonal ramp + RBOB rallying on summer driving prep ($3.70 +2.7% 1D, +17% 1M); EIA STEO May 12 confirmed $89 4Q26 / $79 2027 — official forecaster still below spot. **REVERSIBILITY ASYMMETRIC**: Iran has 2 months. If Shamkhani-Trump frame materializes (sanctions-for-dismantling), Brent gaps -$8-12 to $93-97 within 5 sessions. If Iran rejects formally and ceasefire collapses, Brent gaps +$8-15 toward StanChart $130-145 path. **MARKET REGIME SHIFT — three-force stagflation collision on May 15**: April PPI +1.4% m/m (biggest since Dec 2022, May 13 release) + Warsh Fed-Chair-transition (54-45 May 13, effective May 16) + Trump-Iran reversal = 10Y +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025), 30Y +11bp to 5.121%, S&P -1.24%, NASDAQ -1.54%, Dow lost 50K, gold -1.83%, silver -10.6% one-day. **Crisis component of the day's S&P -1.2% is ~0.5%; rates/Fed is ~0.5%; sympathy with KOSPI/EM is ~0.2%.** Crisis component reverses if Iran responds within 2-month window; rates component does not. **EIA confirmed Hormuz crude/fuel flows dropped ~4 mbpd in March-April**; **Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial print WS408.13 = ~9x pre-war; Week 19 data not yet released through May 16 morning.** Goldman-JPM divergence persists ($85 vs $60). PRIOR (May 15): **C4 BRENT $107.42 / WTI $103.26 — REFUSING TO DEFLATE ON TRUMP-XI JOINT STATEMENT**: Brent +1.6% 1D / +8.1% 1M / +59.3% 3M; WTI +2.1% 1D / +9.1% 1M / +65.7% 3M. **Diplomatic words don't reopen shipping lanes that IRGC mines and small-boat attacks are physically closing.** The Trump-Xi joint statement that Hormuz 'must remain open' is the third repetition of this language since February — strait has remained largely blocked. Primary chain (crisis-linked, ~70%): Hormuz still physically closed at ~30 vessels/day vs 140 pre-war (Aramco CEO Nasser line unchanged); two new kinetic incidents May 14 (Hui Chuan seized off Fujairah, MSV Haj Ali sunk off Oman); Iran proactively cutting production because storage tanks are filling (blockade is binding); EIA May 13 weekly: crude -4.306 Mbbl draw vs -2.1 expected + Cushing -1.702 Mbbl + gasoline -4.084 Mbbl (13th straight); OPEC+ May 3 meeting confirmed only symbolic +206 kbd (most members physically cannot ship); Russian seaborne exports -24% in April from Ukrainian drone strikes; Druzhba flows to Germany halted May 1. Secondary contributor (~30%): refinery utilization +0.5pp to 90.7% (seasonal ramp), distillate slate shift; Trump 172M-barrel SPR emergency exchange actively executing (53.3 Mbbl awarded to Exxon/Trafigura/Marathon). **C5 RBOB GASOLINE -3.2% 1D IS NOT A DEMAND COLLAPSE — IT'S A REFINER SLATE UNWIND**: refiners shifting capacity from gasoline to distillates to chase Europe/Asia diesel-jet shortages; SPR exchange cushioning gasoline feedstock. RBOB-WTI divergence today (-3.2% vs +2.1%) = canary for refinery slate shift. **The paper-physical bifurcation is at cycle extreme**: Brent +1.6% refusing to fall on summit while VIX cycle-low 17.26, S&P/NASDAQ/Dow all at record closes, gold -2.2% (regime-shift unwind), aluminum -8.9% (premium unwind). **One side resolves violently within 5-10 sessions.** **Reversibility**: If Hormuz physically reopens with throughput >50 vessels/day sustained 3+ sessions, Brent could retrace $5-8 to $99-102. If joint statement produces no operational follow-up by May 20 and Brent breaks $108 sustained 2 sessions, StanChart $130-145 path activates. **VLCC TD3C NOW CONFIRMED AT WS459.28 / $467,408/day via Baltic Exchange Week 17** — ~4.2x late-January reading and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. Week 18 partial shows WS408.13 (Lloyd's: 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade') — still ~9x pre-war. **EIA May STEO: Brent ~$106 May/June 2026, $89 4Q26, $79 2027 average**; EIA-Goldman-JPM range ($79-$89-$60) = most divergent bulge-bracket-plus-official-forecaster spread of cycle. Heating Oil $3.95 (+1.2% 1D, +65.3% 3M); European TTF Gas ~€47/MWh +1.47%; AWRP ~1% (8x pre-war). **Trinary band ($100-110) has now held through SEVEN sessions of news escalation**; structural durability signal. PRIOR (May 14): Brent $105.68/$105.87 morning; $108-110 single-session breach not sustained; HEU-via-China door opened by Trump pre-summit. **A3 BRENT $105.68/105.87 MAY 14 MORNING (+0.0%/+0.22% 1D, +11.3% 1M, +56.7% vs Feb 28 pre-crisis ~$73)** — RETRACED from May 13 close $108.05 (single-session breach of $108-110 trigger zone). **$108-110 TRIGGER ZONE BREACH WAS SINGLE-SESSION, NOT SUSTAINED — the StanChart $130-145 cascade path therefore remains DORMANT** (per CRITICAL TAG-DISCIPLINE FLAG 2: trigger remains 'watching', NOT activated). The trinary band ($100-110) has now held through SIX sessions of news escalation (Trump rejection → Aramco 2-5 ships → Sit-Room → Litani → 90% rhetoric → Saudi-strikes-Reuters-amplify → Sledgehammer naming) = meaningful structural durability signal. **C1 PARADOX OF THE DAY: Brent REFUSING TO FALL despite Trump-Xi opening + crisis-trade unwind in defense/tankers/fertilizers** — Lockheed -15% MoM, ITA -4.1% MoM, CF Industries -3.8% 1D, Frontline -3.5% 1D, Cheniere -2.0% 1D, Aluminum -5.6%, RBOB -4.5%, VIX 17.87 (sub-18 on a 1-week-clock-day). **Paper says peace; Physical says blockade. One will resolve violently.** Why disconnect: Hormuz throughput physically at 2-5 ships/day (Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line unchanged); **VLCC TD3C ~$420-462K/day per Lloyd's List May 11 'in unchartered territory'** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K — MAJOR ESCALATION SIGNAL); AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day = 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%, some transits paying up to 10%; TTF €46.63 May 13 (-0.11% 1D); helium 30% global supply disrupted; Aramco Q1 +25% via East-West reroute carries forward; ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8). **B3 EIA MAY STEO PUBLISHED MAY 12 — Brent ~$106 May/June 2026, $89 4Q26, $79 2027 average** (CONFIRMED EIA primary; corrects yesterday's TASS-reported $94.85 = calendar-year 2026 average — both correct on different metrics). EIA: Iraq+Saudi+Kuwait+UAE+Qatar+Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; assumption Hormuz effectively closed until late May, shipping picks up June. EIA $89 4Q26 cuts Goldman-JPM divergence ($85 vs $60) more by trimming Goldman than validating JPM. **EIA-Goldman-JPM range ($79-$89-$60) for late-2026/2027 is now the most divergent bulge-bracket-plus-official-forecaster spread of the cycle.** **BLOOMBERG MAY 13 SATELLITE: Iran's Kharg Island Oil Jetties Empty Again — second satellite snapshot of empty jetties this cycle, operational confirmation of Iranian export collapse without kinetic premium**. EIA additionally reported (Bloomberg May 13): Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026 = official-data confirmation of Lloyd's List operational tracker series. **WTI $101.06 May 14 (+0.0% 1D, +10.7% 1M, +62.1% 3M); Brent-WTI spread $4.6 (compressed sustained).** Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door at Trump-Xi summit Day 1 (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — ToI May 14 liveblog primary) = NEW de-escalation pathway. Vance softer line. Pentagon Sledgehammer naming. Combined picture: Vance keeps diplomatic, Pentagon prepares kinetic, Trump positions in middle with HEU-via-China lever. Reversibility within band symmetric; asymmetric outside. Branch re-pricing: kinetic ~20-25% (DOWN — clock close without strike + Vance softer + HEU-via-China door); extended-economic-warfare ~52-57% (UP modestly — Sledgehammer + IRGC exercise bilateral kinetic-preparedness); negotiated-resolution ~22-27% (UP — China-custodian path explicitly opened). PRIOR (May 13): WTI $100.75 (-1.4% 1D). **THE $108-110 TRIGGER ZONE WAS TESTED AND FAILED — Brent settled at $106.29, BACK BELOW the threshold added to the graph May 12. StanChart $130-145 path therefore remains DORMANT.** The trinary band ($100-110) has now held through FIVE rounds of escalation (Trump rejection → Aramco 2-5 ships → Sit-Room weighing → Litani crossing → 90% rhetoric → Saudi-strikes disclosure) = structural durability, not a transient. Drivers of the retracement: (i) Trump-in-Beijing structural ceiling = summit makes kinetic strike order operationally impossible May 14-16; (ii) EIA cut 2026 Brent forecast to $94.85 (TASS) — official forecaster moved DOWN, pulled away from Goldman's $85 toward JPM's $60; (iii) Aramco Q1 +25% via East-West routing + 'quiet limited Hormuz crude exports resumed' (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West pipeline + UAE bypasses + quiet Hormuz transits — explains why Brent has not gone parabolic despite headline 96% non-Iranian Hormuz collapse; (iv) Iran missile fire rate -92% per JPost + CNN = capability vs willingness gap publicly quantified. **A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS MAY 12) — three-year high; energy contributed >40% of headline gain (+17.9% YoY, gasoline +28.4% YoY)** — first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; closes near-term Fed easing door; US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps); gold +0.8% only on real-yield channel competition. **GOLDMAN $85 vs JPM $60 = STILL $25/BBL DIVERGENCE; EIA $94.85 PULLED OFFICIAL-FORECASTER MEDIAN DOWN — spot $40+ above JPM's 2026 average means market NOT pricing JPM $60 case; either JPM decisively wrong or market in fear-premium regime that reverses on genuine de-escalation.** Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 'market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz disruption persists past mid-June' (CNBC primary); Q1 +25% profit + East-West pipeline pushed to 7 MMbpd capacity with Yanbu exports 3.66-4 MMbpd; ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8). **EIA also confirmed non-Iranian Hormuz crude exports avg ~500,000 bpd since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war (-96%).** PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED: physical (transit collapsed) signals extended disruption; paper-Brent hesitating at $108 threshold partly because of compensating Saudi/UAE non-Hormuz routing + Trump-Xi summit forced pause. Reversibility WITHIN BAND symmetric; asymmetric outside: upside break >$110 sustained 2 sessions = StanChart $130-145 path activates (cascade to inr-usd, nifty-50, sp-500, marine-war-risk-insurance); downside break <$95 sustained 3+ sessions = peace-dividend thesis re-emerges, CF/Nutrien fertilizer trade reverses violently. **TTF Gas ~€46/MWh range (May 11 last live print €46.23, flat-to-slight up into May 13 with no fresh European Council action); AWRP unchanged ~1% (8x pre-war); VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day, Frontline (+1.0% 1D, +10.6% 1M, +28.6% 3M) — insurers + tanker market have NOT bought de-escalation.** Branch read remains: kinetic ~22-27%, extended-economic-warfare ~50-55%, negotiated-resolution ~18-23%. **TRINARY-REGIME HARD FLOOR ESTABLISHED AT $100-105 THROUGH FIVE SESSIONS OF NEWS ESCALATION.** Brent has been pushed up by: Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal ('TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE') + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes + Hormuz transit DETERIORATED to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco CEO Nasser (vs ~6/day May 10) + Goldman Sachs raised 2026 average forecast to **$85/bbl from $77** (+$8) on extended-Hormuz-disruption thesis + VLCC Agios Fanourios I U-turn confidence-collapse signature + new OFAC sanctions wave (12 entities + 3 individuals, Iran→China oil) + second Kharg slick May 11-12 + UAE-Lavan revelation (April 8 strike confirmed retroactive). **GOLDMAN VS JPM $25/BBL 2026 AVERAGE GAP IS THE LARGEST BULGE-BRACKET DIVERGENCE OF CYCLE:** Goldman $85 (with Q2 trimmed to $90 on prior ceasefire optics now stale, Q4 base $71); JPM stays $60 (Natasha Kaneva — bearish base case, 'oil surplus visible in January, likely to persist,' expects voluntary + involuntary production cuts to clear surplus, argues markets are $10/bbl above fair value on Iran fear premium). Spot is $40+ above JPM's 2026 average — market NOT pricing JPM $60 case; either JPM decisively wrong OR market is in fear-premium regime that reverses on any genuine de-escalation. **TTF Gas €46.23 May 11 (+4.74% d/d, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline).** AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day, 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline; VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day, Frontline +11.1% 1M / +32.6% 3M though Frontline -3.4% 1D today on Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply. **BRENT $108-110 BREAK WOULD ACTIVATE STANCHART $130-145 PATH.** Trump China summit May 14-15 forces pause window over 1-week clock close. If Trump returns from Beijing May 15-16 without ordering strikes, Brent could retrace $5-8 toward $97-100; if he orders strikes, Brent +$8-15 within 24h toward StanChart path. **PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED**: physical (Hormuz throughput, AWRP, VLCC rates) all signal extended disruption; paper futures still in oscillating trinary band reflecting Goldman-JPM divergence.

Frontline Q2 contracted TCE $181,700/day (+75% sequential from Q1 $103,500/day) = physical-premium structurally elevated. However, May 21 settled at $103.00-$105 region and May 22 settled at $104.52. The Croft 'Tuesday close vs $112' binary resolves toward the FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE path her May 19 framing anticipated. The StanChart $130-145 escalation path momentum is materially extinguished; Goldman base case ($120 Q3 / $115 Q4 with Hormuz mostly shut) is ITSELF re-pricing because Brent below $105 is below their conditional Hormuz-shut base. Drivers of the reversal: Pakistan-mediated draft text leaked via Al Arabiya May 21-22; Trump 'in no hurry' + 'whatever I want him to do'; Gabbard resignation; structural premium ($30 above $73 baseline) intact even after tail-risk de-pricing $7-8.

recent_signals.headline: **BRENT $103.54 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (+0.9% 1D, -1.5% MoM, +44.8% vs $73 baseline) — FLAT INTO WEEKEND DESPITE HORMUZ DAY 85 + IRGC 25-VESSEL CLAIM. DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE FULLY MATURED. WTI $96.60 (+0.3% 1D, +0.8% MoM). MARKET EXPLICITLY NOT PRICING MAY 24 STRIKE-WINDOW. SATURDAY DIPLOMATIC TAPE BEARISH FOR TAIL-RISK: TRUMP 'EVERY DAY BETTER' + 'SATISFACTORILY HANDLED' URANIUM + MUNIR COAS IN TEHRAN.** (1) **Friday tape flat-into-weekend**: Brent $103.54 (+0.9% 1D), VIX 16.70 cycle low (-13.5% MoM), defense ITA +1.0%, Lockheed +2.0% — only crisis-axis bid left is kinetic-window optionality on Lockheed. Markets pricing the deal as DONE. (2) **Saturday diplomatic tape (Trump 'every day better,' Munir COAS visit Tehran, Baghaei 30-60 day MOU timeline anchor, Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly endorsing Trump+Pakistan) is dominantly bearish for tail risk**: longer-horizon Iranian-side timeline + verbal US accommodation of Mojtaba directive substance ('satisfactorily handled' uranium) + Pakistan COAS escalation of mediation track = deal-architecture-bifurcation regime maturing. (3) **CBS Memorial Day weekend US military/intel cancellation (REPORTED Pentagon-source channel)** is the bullish counter-vector but markets discounted: gold flat at $4,521 (-0.4% 1D, -3.9% MoM), VIX cycle low. **Gold's failure to bid the May 23-25 Memorial Day weekend kinetic window IS the cleanest cross-asset signal that markets do NOT believe Trump pulls the trigger.** (4) **IRGC May 23 claim 25 vessels transited Hormuz** (CLAIMED — contradicted by Bloomberg/Lloyd's/UKMTO continued 'largely frozen' framing; AWRP ~1% holding inconsistent with claim of 25 daily safe transits). Propaganda lock-in for Iran-Oman 'permanent toll mechanism' Rubio's May 21 red line rejected. (5) **India fuel pass-through cascade live**: third petrol/diesel hike May 23 in 8 days (cumulative ₹4.74 petrol, ₹4.82 diesel); petrol > ₹99.51 Delhi, > ₹110 Kolkata, > ₹108 Mumbai. Indian crude basket averaging $113-114/bbl post-war vs $69 pre-war; OMC pass-through now durable. **April 27 fuel-pass-through trigger that fired May 19 is now in confirmed cascade mode Day 4 of 30**. (6) **Frontline Q1 2026 earnings released May 22**: record VLCC TCE $103,500/day, suezmax $72,400, LR2/Aframax $50,700; TCE earnings $424.5M +71% QoQ. **But Q2 booked at 82% of VLCC days at $181,700/day** = physical book structurally bid above current spot. Q2 guidance flagged ballast-day weakness → paper sold the stock -3.4% 1D, BOAT -2.6%. **Physical book is bid, paper is being sold** — cleanest structural-vs-tactical divergence in cycle. (7) **Baltic Exchange Week 21**: VLCC TD3C WS402.5 (-45 pts vs prior Friday); TCE round-trip ~$397,753/day; TD22 (US Gulf → China) TCE ~$106,000/day (fell ~$160K voyage WoW). Atlantic basin softening. **Lloyd's List 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer'** — re-prices freight market structurally. **Sunday-night Asia open + Monday Indian reaction are the next operational binaries**: kinetic event = Brent +$10-15 to $115-120, VIX +5-8 pts, gold +$200-500 in single overnight session; deal-acceptance = Brent -$5-8 to $96-99, breaks sub-$100 trigger 3+ session test starts. **Trigger 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' watching Day 0 of 3** — Croft framing: if fired, Goldman $120 Q3 base case is mathematically foreclosed.
sources: CBS News May 23 liveblog (Trump quotes + Memorial Day cancellation)
Times of Israel May 23 liveblog (Trump '50/50' framing)
Al Jazeera May 22-23 (Munir Tehran + Iranian counter-signal)
Baltic Exchange Week 21 (VLCC TD3C WS402.5 + TD22 TCE)
Frontline plc 2026 Q1 release May 22 (Globe Newswire primary)
Sunday Guardian Live + Goodreturns + Business Today May 23 (India third fuel hike)
Wikipedia 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis (IRGC 25-vessel claim)
Markets dossier 2026-05-23 A, B, C4; Intel dossier 2026-05-23 A1-B6

recent_signals.headline: **BRENT $104.52 MAY 22 (-5.3% CUMULATIVE WINDOW); WTI BROKE $100 TO $99.50 (-6% TWO SESSIONS). CROFT 'TUESDAY CLOSE VS $112' BINARY RESOLVES TO FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE — MAY 21-22 CLOSES DECISIVELY BELOW $108 MATHEMATICALLY FALSIFY MAY 18-19 2-SESSION SUSTAIN RULE. STANCHART $130-145 PATH MOMENTUM EXTINGUISHED. STRUCTURAL PREMIUM $30+ ABOVE $73 BASELINE INTACT.** Brent settled $104.52 May 22 (-5.25% vs $110.32 May 19; -6% cumulative two-session move per Energy Intelligence, Fortune, CNBC); WTI broke $100 to ~$99.50 May 21 (Energy Intelligence). **The Brent breaks $108-110 sustained 2 sessions trigger fire from May 18-19 closes IS NOW MATHEMATICALLY FALSIFIED as a sustain-rule positive**: May 21-22 closes decisively below $108 → revert to one-session positive. The Croft binary resolves toward the FALSE-POSITIVE fire path her May 19 framing anticipated. **Goldman's $120 Q3 / $115 Q4 base case (if Hormuz remains mostly shut) is ALSO re-pricing** because Brent below $105 is below their conditional Hormuz-shut base; market pricing toward 'Hormuz partially reopens within 1-2 months' optionality. **But Brent at $104.52 is still 43% above the $73 pre-crisis baseline** — deal-anticipation re-price compressed tail premium ~$7-8 while preserving structural premium ~$30 above baseline. **Primary chain (~80% crisis-linked)**: Pakistan-mediated draft text leaked via Al Arabiya late May 21 (REPORTED — Saudi state-influenced primary) with ceasefire/Hormuz freedom-of-navigation/sanctions architecture → Trump publicly pivoted from 'an hour away' (May 18) to 'in no hurry' + Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do' → DNI Gabbard resigned May 22 (second senior Iran-skeptic IC departure of cycle) → markets price elevated probability of Hormuz partial reopen within 1-2 months → tail-risk premium compresses ~$7-8/bbl → Brent settles below $108 trigger threshold on May 21-22 closes. **Counter-vector (~20% structural)**: Mojtaba Khamenei's first policy directive (leaked May 21, REPORTED) — enriched uranium stays in Iran — forecloses Trump's May 16 letter nuclear basis; leaked text addresses Hormuz/ceasefire/sanctions but NOT uranium dismantlement; asymmetric path: ceasefire deliverable in days, nuclear deliverable foreclosed. **WTI broke $100 to $99.50 is a meaningful psychological breach**; May 8-9 OPEC+ quota increase to 188K bpd June implementation not formally reversed even as Iranian/OPEC member capacity remains offline; UAE LEAVING OPEC May 1 removes 3M+ bpd theoretical spare capacity (EIA cut OPEC 2027 spare capacity forecast to 2.5M b/d from 3.8M b/d). **Physical-vs-paper bifurcation persists**: paper-crude re-prices the postponement-plus-deal-anticipation tail; physical-shipping rates (Frontline Q2 contracted TCE $181,700/day +75% sequential) confirm physical premium intact; Lloyd's List May 22 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer' — 36% volume cut at extended distances re-prices freight market structurally. **TTF gas €47.69/MWh** (-3.48% 1D, +7.38% 1M, +30.7% YoY) — shallow retracement despite Brent -5.3% = European energy market pricing nuclear-track unresolved + Qatar Ras Laffan 17% force majeure as structural. **WTI-Brent spread compresses to ~$5** (vs ~$6.75 May 19) — consistent with tail-risk-de-pricing dominating. **If Pakistan deal text holds and partial Hormuz reopens, Brent gaps -$10-15 to $89-94.** **If Israel-unilateral kinetic action surfaces (Bibi 'hair on fire' framing per Axios May 20), Brent gaps +$8-15 toward $112-120.** **New trigger added**: 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' watching Day 0 of 3.
sources: CNBC May 21 ('oil prices fall as investors hope for U.S.-Iran deal')
Lloyd's List May 22 ('Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer')
Al Arabiya English May 22 (Pakistan-mediated draft text leak)
Intel dossier 2026-05-22 A1, A2, B1, B7; Markets dossier C1

recent_signals.headline: **BRENT -1.6% TO $110.32 ON TRUMP POSTPONEMENT POST — TAIL-RISK DE-PRICING, STRUCTURAL PREMIUM INTACT; CROFT 'TUESDAY CLOSE VS $112' BINARY RESOLVES TOWARD FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE.** Brent for July delivery fell more than 2% to ~$109.15/barrel Tuesday after Trump's postponement post (CNBC primary, NDTV Profit primary); settled $110.32 (yfinance close-equivalent). WTI -4.7% to $103.57. **This partially inverts the May 18 gap to $111.47 that had triggered the 2-session sustain rule** on Friday-Monday closes. **Croft's 'Tuesday close versus $112' binary now resolves toward the false-positive read**: Brent did not sustain above $112, and the StanChart $130-145 path that activated on Monday's print loses its momentum. Whether the May 18 trigger fire is durable depends on the Tuesday CLOSE — if Brent settles above $108 the 2-session sustain rule mathematically holds; below $108, the sustain-rule fire becomes a 1-session positive and reverts to the trinary $100-110 band. **At $110.32, Brent is still 51.1% above the $73 pre-crisis baseline and 13-18% above the prior trinary $93-97 stabilization band — the postponement re-prices the TAIL, not the structural premium.** Hormuz remains effectively closed (~1-30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war); AWRP 4-6x pre-crisis; Iran IRGC defense systems reportedly activated on Qeshm Island late Monday (REPORTED single-source). OPEC+ 188K bpd June quota increase landing against this tape is symbolic — 30% of OPEC production (~9.7M bpd) is offline due to Hormuz. **WTI moved more on postponement (closer correlation to Hormuz risk-off than to USGC supply); Brent-WTI spread compressed from ~$7 to ~$6.75 = tail-risk-de-pricing reading confirmed.** **Reversibility: HIGH for the postponement component** (the -1.4% reverses if Trump publishes a strike order within 6 days remaining in the NYT May 24 window); **PERMANENT for the structural premium** ($73 → $110 = $37/bbl Hormuz embedded; only Hormuz reopening reverses, not until 2H26 per Baker Hughes). **The defense-derating thesis flagged in yesterday's brief is substantially weakened**: structural-bid trio (XLE +1.9%, Frontline +2.9%, Cheniere +2.4%, Lockheed +2.4% on $879M F-35 contract) all UP on a Brent-down day — premium has migrated out of crude flat-price into ton-miles, LNG basis, and defense backlog.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A1, A2; Markets dossier C1, C2, C4
recent_signals.verification: Brent $110.32 / -1.6% 1D = CONFIRMED operational tape; postponement-driven = CONFIRMED (Trump primary timing); StanChart $130-145 path loses momentum = CONFIRMED analytical reading; structural premium math intact = CONFIRMED ($110.32 vs $73 baseline); structural-bid trio inverse moves = CONFIRMED operational.

recent_signals.headline: **BRENT $111.47 MONDAY MAY 18 (+2.0% 1D, +23.3% 1M, +58.5% 3M vs Feb 28 ~$73) — STANCHART $130-145 PATH ACTIVATES.** Monday intraday range $110.13-$111.99 per Investing.com/Barchart live tape; combined with Friday May 15 confirmed close $109.26, the strict 2-session sustain rule for the May 12-added '$108-110 sustained 2 sessions' trigger is MET. Trigger moves watching → active_fired. **WTI live $108.20 +2.6% per OilPrice.com/Investing.com (yfinance $103.41 -1.9% is stale-comparator artifact — same artifact as aluminum); Brent-WTI spread COMPRESSED to $3-4, not widened.** Drivers (~95% crisis-linked): (i) Sunday May 17 UAE Ministry of Defence — 3 drones entered UAE from western border, 2 intercepted, **1 caused fire at electrical generator outside the inner perimeter of Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant in Al Dhafra** (IAEA expressed 'grave concern'; radiation levels normal; all units operating) — first nuclear-infrastructure-adjacent kinetic incident of cycle; (ii) Saudi defense ministry spokesman Brigadier Turki al-Maliki confirmed Sunday morning **3 drones launched from Iraqi airspace intercepted over Saudi airspace** — ministry 'reserves the right to respond at the appropriate time and place' — first confirmed Saudi territorial breach of cycle; (iii) **Trump-Netanyahu phone call Sunday + Israeli security cabinet meeting + Trump 'clock is ticking' Truth Social post**; (iv) Trump expected to convene NSC Tuesday May 19 (REPORTED via Haaretz May 18 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares for Renewed Iran Strikes') — Tuesday is Day 1 of 6 remaining in NYT 'as soon as next week' window. **CASCADE FIRING SAME DAY**: inr-usd ₹96.29 record low (May 12 trigger fires Day 6 of 5-7 window); Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04%; energy-sector XLE +2.4%; defense-sector ITA -3.2% (NOT bidding on prep news = peace-pricing structural de-rating); MSCI EM -3.4%. **The defense vs oil bifurcation is the cleanest contradiction in today's tape — will resolve violently within 5 trading sessions on Tuesday NSC binary.** Goldman Q3 $120 / Q4 $115 path-dependent case now the active scenario, not the tail; JPM $60 2026 forecast retained, widest bulge-bracket spread of cycle. **Hormuz physical still constrained**: Aramco CEO Nasser line ~30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war; AWRP 3-8% of vessel value vs 0.1-0.15% pre-war (20-50x). **Naqvi-Pezeshkian 90-min Tehran meeting Sunday is the counter-vector** — diplomatic Pakistan-mediation channel Day 2 of 14 framework-deliverable trigger window. **Branch re-pricing**: Branch 1 (kinetic) ~30% (UP from 25-30% on Sunday's dual drone events + Trump NSC meeting); Branch 2 (extended-economic-warfare) ~50% (HOLDS); Branch 3 (de-escalation) ~20% (DOWN — Naqvi mediation real but needs deliverable). **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: Tuesday strike order → Brent +$8-15 to $120-130; Tuesday back-off → Brent -$5-8 to $103-106 (but NOT below $103 absent Hormuz reopening).

recent_signals.headline: **A3 BRENT $105.68/$105.87 MAY 14 MORNING (+0.0%/+0.22% 1D, +11.3% 1M, +56.7% 3M) — RETRACED from May 13 close $108.05 (SINGLE-SESSION breach of $108-110 trigger zone)**. The 2-session sustain rule for StanChart $130-145 cascade UNMET — trigger remains watching, path remains DORMANT. Trinary band ($100-110) has held through SIX sessions of news escalation = structural durability signal. **C1 PARADOX OF THE DAY: Brent REFUSING TO FALL despite Trump-Xi opening + crisis-trade unwind** — Lockheed -15% MoM, ITA -4.1% MoM, CF Industries -3.8% 1D, Frontline -3.5% 1D, Cheniere -2.0% 1D, Aluminum -5.6%, RBOB -4.5%, VIX 17.87 (sub-18 on 1-week-clock-day). Paper says peace; Physical says blockade. Drivers of paper-Brent anchored above $105: (i) Hormuz throughput physically at 2-5 ships/day (Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 unchanged); (ii) **VLCC TD3C ~$420-462K/day per Lloyd's List May 11 'in unchartered territory'** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K — MAJOR escalation signal); (iii) AWRP ~1% (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%); (iv) helium 30% global supply disrupted; (v) **Bloomberg May 13 satellite: Kharg Island Oil Jetties Empty Again** = operational confirmation of Iranian export collapse; (vi) **EIA confirmed Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026** (Bloomberg May 13). **B3 EIA MAY STEO MAY 12 (CONFIRMED PRIMARY): Brent ~$106 May-June 2026, $89 4Q26, $79 2027 average** (corrects yesterday's TASS-reported $94.85 = calendar-year 2026 average — both correct on different metrics). EIA: Iraq+Saudi+Kuwait+UAE+Qatar+Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; assumption Hormuz effectively closed until late May, shipping picks up June. EIA $89 4Q26 cuts Goldman-JPM divergence ($85 vs $60) more by trimming Goldman than validating JPM. **EIA-Goldman-JPM range ($79-$89-$60) for late-2026/2027 = most divergent bulge-bracket-plus-official-forecaster spread of cycle.** WTI $101.06 May 14 (+0.0% 1D, +10.7% 1M, +62.1% 3M); Brent-WTI spread $4.6 (compressed sustained). **Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door** at Trump-Xi summit Day 1 (CONFIRMED RHETORIC ToI May 14 liveblog primary) = NEW de-escalation pathway in different jurisdiction. Vance softer line. Pentagon Sledgehammer naming (REPORTED). **Either Brent gaps -$8-12 to $93-97 Friday on Trump-Xi deliverable, OR risk assets reverse Friday-Monday on no-deal — bifurcation will not last another full week.**

Drivers of retracement: Trump-in-Beijing summit-pause ceiling (May 14-16 = kinetic strike order operationally impossible); EIA cut 2026 forecast to $94.85 (TASS, official forecaster moving DOWN, away from Goldman $85); Aramco Q1 +25% on East-West rerouting + Yanbu exports 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May + ADNOC quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports (World Oil May 8); Iran missile fire rate -92% (JPost + CNN intel) = capability ≠ willingness ≠ logistics. **April CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS May 12, three-year high since May 2023, +0.6% MoM; CORE +2.8% YoY / +0.4% MoM; energy contributed >40% of headline gain, +17.9% YoY; gasoline +28.4% YoY; shelter +0.6%; BLS itself flagged inflation 'a problem beyond Iran war impacts')** — first cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through; closes near-term Fed easing door; US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps). Goldman-JPM $25/bbl 2026 average gap intact; EIA $94.85 pulled official-forecaster median DOWN. Reversibility: upside break >$110 sustained 2 sessions = StanChart $130-145 activates; downside break <$95 sustained 3+ sessions = peace-dividend re-emerges.

recent_signals.headline: BRENT $105.58 MAY 12 (+1.3% 1D, +6.3% 1M, +56.4% vs Feb 28 ~$73); intel A11 cites $104.97 May 12 intraday print after $101.73 May 8 close. TRINARY-REGIME HARD FLOOR $100-105 ESTABLISHED THROUGH 5 SESSIONS OF NEWS ESCALATION. WTI $99.65 (+1.6% 1D). Drivers: Trump REJECTED Iran counter-proposal ('TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE'/'garbage') + WH Sit-Room May 11 weighing renewed strikes (NO ORDERS) + Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 Hormuz 2-5 ships/day DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day + Goldman raised 2026 average $77→$85 (+$8) on extended-disruption thesis + JPM stays $60 (Natasha Kaneva — 'oil surplus visible January, likely persists'; markets $10/bbl above fair value on Iran fear premium) = LARGEST BULGE-BRACKET DIVERGENCE OF CYCLE; VLCC Agios Fanourios I U-turn at Gulf of Oman (Iraqi Basra crude bound Vietnam Nghi Son) without crossing US blockade = confidence-collapse signature even for compliant non-Iranian VLCCs; NEW OFAC SANCTIONS WAVE (12 entities + 3 individuals Iran→China oil); SECOND KHARG SLICK MAY 11-12 (~12-20 sq km); UAE LAVAN APRIL 8 RETROACTIVELY CONFIRMED (55,000 bpd offline months); TTF Gas €46.23 May 11 (+4.74% d/d, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline); AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M (8x pre-war); CHAIN 'Brent <$105 sustained 5+ sessions' FAILED on May 12 break above $105. Trump China summit May 14-15 = forced pause window. Goldman Q4 2026 base case $71 = bullish-case requires 'back half of year' retrace; StanChart $130-145 path activated only if Brent breaks $108-110 sustained. Spot $40+ above JPM's 2026 average means market NOT pricing JPM $60 case; either JPM decisively wrong OR market is in fear-premium regime that reverses on any genuine de-escalation.
sources: CNBC (Aramco CEO Nasser quotes + JPM Kaneva)
WSJ primary (UAE Lavan + Iran HEU terms)
Intel dossier sections A11, B3, B4, B6, B7, B12, B14, B17

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY (US, India, most W Europe markets CLOSED) — FRIDAY MAY 8 CLOSE $101.29-$101.73 STILL OPERATIVE. Two days of unsettled news flow accumulated against frozen prices: bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike (NEW — first commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM May 5; first inside Qatari EEZ; UKMTO Warning notice; NO claim of responsibility); CENTCOM precision tally 58 redirected + 4 disabled since April 13 (UPDATED — refines yesterday's >70-tankers-held framing); Qatar PM al-Thani Miami meeting Saturday + Axios scoop on Qatar-IRGC channel (UPDATED-MAJOR); Bahrain 41 IRGC-linked detentions (NEW); KHARG ISLAND SATELLITE-CONFIRMED OIL SLICK (NEW — first hard-data signal blockade biting at production-export interface; CAUSE CONTESTED — Iran blames European tanker waste, Fox-quoted experts suggest storage-overflow dumping); UAE air defense engagement scale refined (UPDATED — 2 ballistic + 3 drones, 3 wounded). Monday May 11 Asian open is first repricing event. Expect Brent gap-up of $2-4 if no countervailing diplomatic news Sunday. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day baseline; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 4 of 10 — failing harder. Goldman Q2 $90 target stretched against zero-transit + 1,500-vessel data + Sunday Doha strike — likely revises higher Monday morning if trinary regime persists.
sources: Intel dossier Section A1, A2, A3, A5, A11, B1, B2, B4
UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
recent_signals.verification: Friday May 8 close = CONFIRMED yfinance multi-source ($101.29-$101.73 settlement vs intraday); Sunday-morning Doha strike = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary, multi-source); perpetrator UNKNOWN; CENTCOM 58/4 = CONFIRMED ACTION; Qatar back-channel = REPORTED via Axios primary scoop; Kharg slick = CONFIRMED imagery, CONTESTED on cause; UAE engagement = CONFIRMED multi-source; AWRP unchanged = CONFIRMED multi-source

recent_signals.headline: BRENT CLOSE $101.73 MAY 8 (+1.66% 1D); FRIDAY RANGE $96.07 → $103.96 = TRINARY-REGIME SIGNATURE OSCILLATION; SUB-$105 CHAIN DAY 3 INTACT BUT OSCILLATING. The Friday tape low → high → close sequence ($96 → $104 → $101) is the cleanest signature of a market not believing either binary side (deal signs OR full kinetic) and pricing the trinary middle. The $96 intraday low reflects MOU-progress hopes (May 7); the $104 May 8 intraday high reflects second day of US tanker strikes (M/T Sea Star III, M/T Sevda) + CENTCOM disclosure of 70-tanker blockade scale + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz speech. Goldman Q2 $90 target now stretched against Lloyd's List zero-transit print May 7 + 1,500-vessel Persian Gulf standstill — likely revises higher if trinary regime extends past May 21. Standard Chartered $130-145 path remains conditional on MOU collapse. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day, 6-8x pre-war norm — insurers have NOT bought peace story; physical-paper bifurcation deepened.
sources: Goldman Sachs Q2 research note (institutional anchor)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED price tape multi-source ($101.73 settlement vs $101.29 yfinance snapshot — same signal); intraday range $96.07-$103.96 = CONFIRMED Friday tape; trinary-regime mechanism = CONFIRMED via concurrent CENTCOM blockade scale + Mokhber speech + Hormuz operational data

recent_signals.headline: BRENT COLLAPSED -7% MAY 6 ON AXIOS MOU SCOOP TO ~$103; TRADING $101.95 MAY 7 MORNING (+0.5% MODEST STABILIZATION); SUB-$115 CHAIN COMPLETED; SUB-$105 CHAIN DAY 1. World Bank April 28 forecasts Brent averaging $86/bbl in 2026 vs $69 in 2025 — 'largest energy price surge since 2022.' Goldman Q2 cut to $90 (from $99); JPMorgan $150 retained ONLY in kinetic-restart scenario. May 4 kinetic pop + pre-May-4 baseline both broken. 48-hour Iran response window closes ~May 8 evening — binary test for further leg down to $86-90 (MOU signs) vs +$8-15 bounce to $115+ (Iran rejects).

historical_summaries.summary: 11 signals. Oil surged faster than during any other conflict in recent history following US-Israel strikes on Iran and Iranian retaliation across Gulf s | Brent crossed $100/bbl for first time in four years as Hormuz closure entered second week with no resolution in sight | Crude topped $112 after Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields and drones struck two Kuwaiti refineries
45 signals. Brent crashed -14 | Brent continued sliding to $101 | Brent-WTI spread collapsed from $18-20 to ~$2-3 — market saying 'we no longer believe Gulf supply permanently disrupted
